mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5%, it signals a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 5% but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such events over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or below 0.25% indicates extreme monetary accommodation, compressing discount rates and encouraging equity investment, generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25% but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and industrial production signal a broad-based economic recovery in both construction and manufacturing, boosting confidence and raising equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and INDPRO show month-over-month increase
failure_condition: If both series rise month-over-month but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production growth exceeding 1% signals strong manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as production capacity expands.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth exceeds 1% MoM but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in payroll employment and retail sales reflect a virtuous cycle of job creation and consumer spending, reinforcing positive earnings expectations and lifting equity prices within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If both employment and retail sales rise MoM but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of events over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year CPI inflation below 2% signals that the Federal Reserve is unlikely to tighten policy in the near term, maintaining accommodative financial conditions that support equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If inflation is below 2% YoY but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate, regardless of starting level, indicates improving labor market conditions that consistently boost investor confidence and lead to positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the fed funds rate is extremely low (<0.5%) and unemployment is elevated (>5%), markets price in rapid economic normalization and policy support, driving sustained equity gains over a 60-day horizon as recovery expectations solidify.
failure_condition: If both conditions hold but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such episodes over any 3-year rolling window.
